primary_factors: Temps well above freezing during bus/commute window (~40–45°F) with rising trend → no refreeze/black-ice mechanism
No precipitation expected (0% snow/rain signal; no accumulation) and good visibility
Road network and lots reported/assessed as clear; no plow operations needed before routes
No neighboring-district closure domino signals; no Rockford Public Schools closure/delay messaging found; minimal community buzz
Power grid conditions normal with low statewide outage levels and no ice-loading threat (only isolated outage tail risk)
decision_rationale: All six expert inputs align on a non-winter, low-risk Monday in Rockford: no accumulating snow/ice, temps safely above freezing through the morning commute, and visibility not degraded. Safety and infrastructure assessments both indicate roads/parking lots should be clear with only a very small tail risk of isolated slick spots from prior wet areas—unlikely to justify a district-wide closure given the temperature margin above 32°F. News intelligence shows no closure momentum (no neighboring districts closing, no official Rockford signal, no social buzz), and power-grid conditions look stable with no storm driver. The only meaningful ways this forecast could be wrong are non-weather operational issues (localized power/facility failure) or an unexpected, highly localized slick-spot/incident—events that more often cause a targeted delay than a full snow day. Recommendation: OPEN with routine monitoring rather than delay/closure.

collaboration.summary: Agents reached consensus after 2 round(s) with 2.0% spread. Average probability: 2%.
